title: oxalis acetosella var. parviflora
intro: Oxalis acetosella var. parviflora, also known as small-leaved wood sorrel, is a variety of the wood sorrel plant that is characterized by its small white flowers and narrow, elliptical leaves with a sour taste. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include its ability to act as a diuretic, anti-inflammatory, and antioxidant. The health benefits of Oxalis acetosella var. parviflora include its potential to help alleviate urinary tract issues, reduce inflammation, and protect against oxidative stress. In herbal preparations, the plant is often used to make teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be consumed to harness its medicinal properties.
